trans-unit.source: Exercise 1: Creating an overview map of the health system and vaccination coverage
trans-unit.target: Exercise 1: Creating an overview map of the health system and vaccination coverage

trans-unit.source: %% MISSING: - MAKE ENRICHED DATASET PERMANENT %% - BASEMAP %% - SMALL SELECTION EXPORT/QUERIES? %% - PROJECTIONS %% - ADD SMALLER EASIER EXERCISES EARLIER (INCLUDING IMPORTING DATA SO HERE WE CAN INTRODUCE THE PROJECT HOME) %% - ADD MORE PICTURES %% - REMOVE STEPS TO CLEAN HEALTHSITES AS HEALTHSITES WITHOUT INFORMATION COULD STILL BE GOOD; E.G., TO REACH OUT %% - ADD STEP FOR COUNT POINTS IN POLYGONS FOR HEALTHSITES %% - ADD STEP FOR JOIN BY LOCATION %% - MAKE THEM PLAN MEASLES VACCINATION CAMPAIGN %% - WHERE TO PUT VACCINATION CENTERS WITH HOW MUCH POP TO SERVE %% - WHERE TO DEPLOY MOBILE VACCINATION CENTERS -> DIGITISATION %% - IM COORDINATION INSRTUCTED TO GET DATA ON HEALTHSITE CAPACITIES %% - MAKE THEM DOWNLOAD HDX %% - ADD SMALL STEPS UNDER DROPDOWN, AS NOTES, ETC.
trans-unit.target: %% MISSING: - MAKE ENRICHED DATASET PERMANENT %% - BASEMAP %% - SMALL SELECTION EXPORT/QUERIES? %% - PROJECTIONS %% - ADD SMALLER EASIER EXERCISES EARLIER (INCLUDING IMPORTING DATA SO HERE WE CAN INTRODUCE THE PROJECT HOME) %% - ADD MORE PICTURES %% - REMOVE STEPS TO CLEAN HEALTHSITES AS HEALTHSITES WITHOUT INFORMATION COULD STILL BE GOOD; E.G., TO REACH OUT %% - ADD STEP FOR COUNT POINTS IN POLYGONS FOR HEALTHSITES %% - ADD STEP FOR JOIN BY LOCATION %% - MAKE THEM PLAN MEASLES VACCINATION CAMPAIGN %% - WHERE TO PUT VACCINATION CENTERS WITH HOW MUCH POP TO SERVE %% - WHERE TO DEPLOY MOBILE VACCINATION CENTERS -> DIGITISATION %% - IM COORDINATION INSRTUCTED TO GET DATA ON HEALTHSITE CAPACITIES %% - MAKE THEM DOWNLOAD HDX %% - ADD SMALL STEPS UNDER DROPDOWN, AS NOTES, ETC.

trans-unit.source: Background
trans-unit.target: Background

trans-unit.source: Over the past month, health authorities in Chad have reported a surge in measles cases, particularly in Mandoul, Mayo-Kebbi Est, and Logone Oriental regions. The surveillance unit has provided line-list data and existing health facility data. Your first task is to create a base map showing health facility distribution and classify them by service capacity to understand the available response infrastructure.
trans-unit.target: Over the past month, health authorities in Chad have reported a surge in measles cases, particularly in Mandoul, Mayo-Kebbi Est, and Logone Oriental regions. The surveillance unit has provided line-list data and existing health facility data. Your first task is to create a base map showing health facility distribution and classify them by service capacity to understand the available response infrastructure.

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.target: Dataset name

trans-unit.target: Original title

trans-unit.target: Downloaded from

trans-unit.target: `tcd_admbnda_adm0_20250212_AB.shp` (Polygons)

trans-unit.target: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/chad-roads-osm-ministry-of-transport)

trans-unit.source: Healthsite Capacities
trans-unit.target: Healthsite Capacities

trans-unit.source: HeiGIT
trans-unit.target: HeiGIT

trans-unit.source: This is a fictional dataset generated for the purpose of this exercise.
trans-unit.target: This is a fictional dataset generated for the purpose of this exercise.

trans-unit.source: Measles vaccination coverage
trans-unit.target: Measles vaccination coverage

trans-unit.source: HeiGIT
trans-unit.target: HeiGIT

trans-unit.source: This is a fictional dataset generated for the purpose of this exercise.
trans-unit.target: This is a fictional dataset generated for the purpose of this exercise.

trans-unit.source: In this exercise, we will download real datasets from the [Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX)](humdata.org) to identify and analyse relevant information. However, the Healthsite Capacities and Vaccination Coverage datasets used here are fictional and created solely for training purposes. They do not represent real-world data.
trans-unit.target: In this exercise, we will download real datasets from the [Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX)](humdata.org) to identify and analyse relevant information. However, the Healthsite Capacities and Vaccination Coverage datasets used here are fictional and created solely for training purposes. They do not represent real-world data.

trans-unit.source: __Download all datasets provided by HeiGIT [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/public_health/GIS_Training_Public_Health.zip) and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__
trans-unit.target: __Download all datasets provided by HeiGIT [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/public_health/GIS_Training_Public_Health.zip) and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__

trans-unit.source: Tasks
trans-unit.target: Tasks

trans-unit.source: Task 1: Setting up the folder structure and creating a new QGIS project
trans-unit.target: Task 1: Setting up the folder structure and creating a new QGIS project

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Standard Folder Structure :class: tip
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Standard Folder Structure :class: tip

trans-unit.source: The single most important geodata management practice is to use a standardised folder structure that contains all parts of the QGIS project. We will save all of our data that we use or create within our QGIS project inside of this folder structure. The paths from a QGIS project to the geodata are by default relative. This means when the data and the project are in a fixed folder structure, you can move the whole structure without impacting the QGIS project or the paths to the data.
trans-unit.target: The single most important geodata management practice is to use a standardised folder structure that contains all parts of the QGIS project. We will save all of our data that we use or create within our QGIS project inside of this folder structure. The paths from a QGIS project to the geodata are by default relative. This means when the data and the project are in a fixed folder structure, you can move the whole structure without impacting the QGIS project or the paths to the data.

trans-unit.source: A standard folder structure has two principal advantages:
trans-unit.target: A standard folder structure has two principal advantages:

trans-unit.source: If we share the whole project folder, we can expect the project to run without problems on a different computer.
trans-unit.target: If we share the whole project folder, we can expect the project to run without problems on a different computer.

trans-unit.source: The folder structure supports the proper organisation of project data and helps ensure the QGIS project will work as intended. :::
trans-unit.target: The folder structure supports the proper organisation of project data and helps ensure the QGIS project will work as intended. :::

trans-unit.source: Create a new folder on your computer with the name "GIS_Training_Public_Health_Day_1-2". In the folder create the following folder structure:
trans-unit.target: Create a new folder on your computer with the name "GIS_Training_Public_Health_Day_1-2". In the folder create the following folder structure:

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a new project.
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and create a new project.

trans-unit.source: Save the project via `Project` → `Save As...`. Navigate to the folder for this training and save it in the `/project` subfolder. Give it a name (e.g., `GIS_Training_Public_Health_Part_1`) and click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Save the project via `Project` → `Save As...`. Navigate to the folder for this training and save it in the `/project` subfolder. Give it a name (e.g., `GIS_Training_Public_Health_Part_1`) and click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Now we should set up the Project CRS.
trans-unit.target: Now we should set up the Project CRS.

trans-unit.source: In the bottom right corner of the QGIS window, click on the ![](/fig/3.40_projection_icon.png) Projection icon. Let's choose a metric CRS that depicts Chad without distorting too much. For this exercise, we will use __"Albers Equal Area Conic" (EPSG: 102022)__. In the `Filter` bar, enter the name or the EPSG number. The CRS should appear in the "Predefined Coordinate Reference Systems" Box. Select it and click `Apply` and `OK`. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_8_pub_health_1_project_crs.png
trans-unit.target: In the bottom right corner of the QGIS window, click on the ![](/fig/3.40_projection_icon.png) Projection icon. Let's choose a metric CRS that depicts Chad without distorting too much. For this exercise, we will use __"Albers Equal Area Conic" (EPSG: 102022)__. In the `Filter` bar, enter the name or the EPSG number. The CRS should appear in the "Predefined Coordinate Reference Systems" Box. Select it and click `Apply` and `OK`. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_8_pub_health_1_project_crs.png

trans-unit.source: Setting the Project CRS in QGIS :::
trans-unit.target: Setting the Project CRS in QGIS :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{admonition} How to choose the correct CRS :class: dropdown, note
trans-unit.target: ::::{admonition} How to choose the correct CRS :class: dropdown, note

trans-unit.source: Choosing a suitable Coordinate Reference System for your GIS project is crucial. In general, you want to use a CRS that best displays your area of interest. You want to choose a Secondly, you need to think about what kind of calculations you plan to do in your project. CRS exist in two varieties: geographic and metric coordinate reference systems. Geographic CRS use longitude and latitude (degrees) as their coordinates and units of measurements. These are angular measurements and don't depict distances linearly. Metric coordinate reference systems use coordinates that are given in __linear units of measurements__ (such as meters), which means they represent actual distances on the Earth's surface. Metric coordinate reference systems, such as UTM, are designed for local and regional mapping and can introduce distortion when applied to larger areas. For very large regions, projections like Albers Equal Area or Mercator are often used to reduce distortion
trans-unit.target: Choosing a suitable Coordinate Reference System for your GIS project is crucial. In general, you want to use a CRS that best displays your area of interest. You want to choose a Secondly, you need to think about what kind of calculations you plan to do in your project. CRS exist in two varieties: geographic and metric coordinate reference systems. Geographic CRS use longitude and latitude (degrees) as their coordinates and units of measurements. These are angular measurements and don't depict distances linearly. Metric coordinate reference systems use coordinates that are given in __linear units of measurements__ (such as meters), which means they represent actual distances on the Earth's surface. Metric coordinate reference systems, such as UTM, are designed for local and regional mapping and can introduce distortion when applied to larger areas. For very large regions, projections like Albers Equal Area or Mercator are often used to reduce distortion

trans-unit.source: If you wish to perform distance calculations, you should use a metric CRS.
trans-unit.target: If you wish to perform distance calculations, you should use a metric CRS.

trans-unit.source: If you want to know more about Projections and Coordinate Reference Systems, check out this __[module chapter](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_projections.md)__.
trans-unit.target: If you want to know more about Projections and Coordinate Reference Systems, check out this __[module chapter](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_projections.md)__.

trans-unit.source: The project CRS determines which coordinate reference system is being used to display the geodata on the QGIS map canvas. However, it does not change the CRS of layers. Each layer, or dataset, is encoded with a CRS. QGIS reprojects these layers "on the fly" to display layers with different CRS on the map canvas. This does not change the units of measurements or distortion of the actual layers. To perform distance calculations, you will need to [reproject the layer](/content/wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md) to a metric coordinate reference system.
trans-unit.target: The project CRS determines which coordinate reference system is being used to display the geodata on the QGIS map canvas. However, it does not change the CRS of layers. Each layer, or dataset, is encoded with a CRS. QGIS reprojects these layers "on the fly" to display layers with different CRS on the map canvas. This does not change the units of measurements or distortion of the actual layers. To perform distance calculations, you will need to [reproject the layer](/content/wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md) to a metric coordinate reference system.

trans-unit.source: Setting the Project CRS to your desired CRS can help you choosing the correct CRS quicker when running algorithms. ::: %% SET UP PROJECT HOME IN NEXT EXERCISE
trans-unit.target: Setting the Project CRS to your desired CRS can help you choosing the correct CRS quicker when running algorithms. ::: %% SET UP PROJECT HOME IN NEXT EXERCISE

trans-unit.source: Task 2: Downloading the relevant data
trans-unit.target: Task 2: Downloading the relevant data

trans-unit.source: In your browser, head over to __humdata.org__
trans-unit.target: In your browser, head over to __humdata.org__

trans-unit.source: Search for the following datasets
trans-unit.target: Search for the following datasets

trans-unit.source: Chad Administrative Boundaries (OCHA): ADM0, ADM1, ADM2
trans-unit.target: Chad Administrative Boundaries (OCHA): ADM0, ADM1, ADM2

trans-unit.source: Chad Health Facilities (OpenStreetMap Export)
trans-unit.target: Chad Health Facilities (OpenStreetMap Export)

trans-unit.source: Chad Roads (OCHA) :::{figure} /fig/en_m3_ex_8_public_health_part_1_hdx_search.png
trans-unit.target: Chad Roads (OCHA) :::{figure} /fig/en_m3_ex_8_public_health_part_1_hdx_search.png

trans-unit.source: ::: 3. Download the layers. - On the download page, you can usually select different data formats. The formats are indicated by their file endings (e.g., `.shp`, `.gpkg`, `.gdb`). - Sometimes the data is still zipped, so the file extension isn’t visible. - Choose the following formats: - Chad Administrative Boundaries (OCHA): __Shapefile__ - Chad Health Facilities (OpenStreetMap Export): __GeoPackage__ for __Points__, we don't need the polygons information for this example - Chad Roads (OCHA): __Shapefile__ :::{figure} /fig/en_m3_ex_8_public_health_part_1_hdx_data_formats.png --- name: en_m3_ex_8_public_health_part_1_hdx_data_formats width: 600 px --- ::: 4. Unzip the folders and make sure to save them in the standard folder structure into the `data/input/`-folder.
trans-unit.target: ::: 3. Download the layers. - On the download page, you can usually select different data formats. The formats are indicated by their file endings (e.g., `.shp`, `.gpkg`, `.gdb`). - Sometimes the data is still zipped, so the file extension isn’t visible. - Choose the following formats: - Chad Administrative Boundaries (OCHA): __Shapefile__ - Chad Health Facilities (OpenStreetMap Export): __GeoPackage__ for __Points__, we don't need the polygons information for this example - Chad Roads (OCHA): __Shapefile__ :::{figure} /fig/en_m3_ex_8_public_health_part_1_hdx_data_formats.png --- name: en_m3_ex_8_public_health_part_1_hdx_data_formats width: 600 px --- ::: 4. Unzip the folders and make sure to save them in the standard folder structure into the `data/input/`-folder.

trans-unit.source: %% ADD IMAGE AND EXPAND THIS SECTION: DONE
trans-unit.target: %% ADD IMAGE AND EXPAND THIS SECTION: DONE

trans-unit.source: Task 3: Importing the datasets
trans-unit.target: Task 3: Importing the datasets

trans-unit.source: In your QGIS, project, [import the following datasets](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop) via drag-and-drop:
trans-unit.target: In your QGIS, project, [import the following datasets](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop) via drag-and-drop:

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Importing Shapefiles into a QGIS project via drag-and-drop
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Importing Shapefiles into a QGIS project via drag-and-drop
:::

trans-unit.source: %% THIS STEP NEEDS REVISING: THEY ARE DOWNLOADING THE DATASET THEMSELVES AS GEOPACKAGE
trans-unit.target: %% THIS STEP NEEDS REVISING: THEY ARE DOWNLOADING THE DATASET THEMSELVES AS GEOPACKAGE

trans-unit.source: %% THE FOLLOWING SECTION IS NOT NEEDED THEN?
trans-unit.target: %% THE FOLLOWING SECTION IS NOT NEEDED THEN?

trans-unit.source: Imported files __are not saved within__ the QGIS project. If you move or delete the original file, QGIS will no longer find the respective dataset.
trans-unit.target: Imported files __are not saved within__ the QGIS project. If you move or delete the original file, QGIS will no longer find the respective dataset.

trans-unit.source: Task 4: The layers panel and the layer concept
trans-unit.target: Task 4: The layers panel and the layer concept

trans-unit.source: Once we've imported all the relevant layers, lets start by arranging the layers logically so we can work with them more easily. On the left, there is the `Layers`-panel. Here you can see all the datasets we've imported so far.
trans-unit.target: Once we've imported all the relevant layers, lets start by arranging the layers logically so we can work with them more easily. On the left, there is the `Layers`-panel. Here you can see all the datasets we've imported so far.

trans-unit.source: QGIS displays geodata in layers, where each dataset is represented in one layer. The layers are stacked on top of each other.
trans-unit.target: QGIS displays geodata in layers, where each dataset is represented in one layer. The layers are stacked on top of each other.

trans-unit.source: Lets arrange the layers so we work with them more easily:
trans-unit.target: Lets arrange the layers so we work with them more easily:

trans-unit.source: The ADM0-layer should go at the bottom, followed by ADM1, then ADM2.
trans-unit.target: The ADM0-layer should go at the bottom, followed by ADM1, then ADM2.

trans-unit.source: Then we can add the road network.
trans-unit.target: Then we can add the road network.

trans-unit.source: The healthsites should go on top.
trans-unit.target: The healthsites should go on top.

trans-unit.source: Let's add a basemap:
trans-unit.target: Let's add a basemap:

trans-unit.source: In the file browser, scrool down until you see `XYZ-Tiles`
trans-unit.target: In the file browser, scrool down until you see `XYZ-Tiles`

trans-unit.source: Uncollapse it and <kbd>double-click</kbd> on `OpenStreetMap`. A new layer will be added to your layers-panel, usually at the bottom. Make sure the layer sits at the bottom of the layers panel so all your other layers are visible.
trans-unit.target: Uncollapse it and <kbd>double-click</kbd> on `OpenStreetMap`. A new layer will be added to your layers-panel, usually at the bottom. Make sure the layer sits at the bottom of the layers panel so all your other layers are visible.

trans-unit.source: Your QGIS window should look similar to this (with different colours for the layers).
trans-unit.target: Your QGIS window should look similar to this (with different colours for the layers).

trans-unit.source: Let's investigate the layers that we have added so far. Each vector layer has an attribute table, where each row represents a geometric feature on the map canvas.
trans-unit.target: Let's investigate the layers that we have added so far. Each vector layer has an attribute table, where each row represents a geometric feature on the map canvas.

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on the ADM2 layer in the layers panel on the left → `Open Attribute Table`.
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on the ADM2 layer in the layers panel on the left → `Open Attribute Table`.

trans-unit.source: A new window will open. This is the attribute table. It shows the vector layer in a tabular format, allowing you to see the attribute values, sort the table, and edit the values using the tools in the top bar.
trans-unit.target: A new window will open. This is the attribute table. It shows the vector layer in a tabular format, allowing you to see the attribute values, sort the table, and edit the values using the tools in the top bar.

trans-unit.source: Take a look at the different columns in the attribute table. What do they show?
trans-unit.target: Take a look at the different columns in the attribute table. What do they show?

trans-unit.source: Try sorting the attribute table by clicking on the ![](/fig/sort.png)
trans-unit.target: Try sorting the attribute table by clicking on the ![](/fig/sort.png)

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute tables for the layers `hotosm_tcd_health_facilities_points_gpkg` and `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB.shp` familiarise yourself with the data.
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute tables for the layers `hotosm_tcd_health_facilities_points_gpkg` and `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB.shp` familiarise yourself with the data.

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on each layer and select
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on each layer and select

trans-unit.source: Task 5: Joining Vaccination Coverage Data with administrative boundaries
trans-unit.target: Task 5: Joining Vaccination Coverage Data with administrative boundaries

trans-unit.source: %% ADD A DISCLAIMER MAKING TRAINEES THINK WHERE THE DATA CAME FROM
trans-unit.target: %% ADD A DISCLAIMER MAKING TRAINEES THINK WHERE THE DATA CAME FROM

trans-unit.source: In our `data/input`-folder, we can find a csv file called `vaccination_coverage_adm2`. This file includes the vaccination coverage of both the mcv1 and mcv2 vaccine. Thankfully, the dataset includes the district name (`amd2_name`) and the adm2 pcode. With this information, we can perform a [non-spatial join](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.html) in order to add the vaccination coverage data to our district boundaries layer (adm2).
trans-unit.target: In our `data/input`-folder, we can find a csv file called `vaccination_coverage_adm2`. This file includes the vaccination coverage of both the mcv1 and mcv2 vaccine. Thankfully, the dataset includes the district name (`amd2_name`) and the adm2 pcode. With this information, we can perform a [non-spatial join](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.html) in order to add the vaccination coverage data to our district boundaries layer (adm2).

trans-unit.source: %% ADD ADM2 PCODE TO DATASET AND HAVE THE ADM2 NAMES IN ENGLISH SO THEY CAN'T JOIN WITH ADM2 NAMES BUT USE PCODES: DONE
trans-unit.target: %% ADD ADM2 PCODE TO DATASET AND HAVE THE ADM2 NAMES IN ENGLISH SO THEY CAN'T JOIN WITH ADM2 NAMES BUT USE PCODES: DONE

trans-unit.source: Import the `vaccination_coverage_adm2` into your QGIS project:
trans-unit.target: Import the `vaccination_coverage_adm2` into your QGIS project:

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Delimited Text Layer...`
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Delimited Text Layer...`

trans-unit.source: To the right of the `File name`-field, click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three points and navigate to the `data/input/vaccination_coverage_adm2.csv` file and click `Open`.
trans-unit.target: To the right of the `File name`-field, click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three points and navigate to the `data/input/vaccination_coverage_adm2.csv` file and click `Open`.

trans-unit.source: In the import window, you will see sample data in the sample data field. Take a look at the columns and data available. What kind of data is present in each column?
trans-unit.target: In the import window, you will see sample data in the sample data field. Take a look at the columns and data available. What kind of data is present in each column?

trans-unit.source: Unfortunately, there are no columns with the coordinates for the individual healthsites in this data table. Under `Geometry Definition` select `No geometry (attribute only table)`.
trans-unit.target: Unfortunately, there are no columns with the coordinates for the individual healthsites in this data table. Under `Geometry Definition` select `No geometry (attribute only table)`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Add`. The layer will appear in your layers tab as a data table, but will not be shown in the map canvas. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_8_pub_health_1_add_vacc_coverage_csv.png
trans-unit.target: Click `Add`. The layer will appear in your layers tab as a data table, but will not be shown in the map canvas. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_8_pub_health_1_add_vacc_coverage_csv.png

trans-unit.source: Investigate the new vaccination coverage further:
trans-unit.target: Investigate the new vaccination coverage further:

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the new layer and open the attribute table. What information is available? How is the table structured. We can see that we are able to use the column `ADM2_PCODE` to perform a [non-spatial join]
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the new layer and open the attribute table. What information is available? How is the table structured. We can see that we are able to use the column `ADM2_PCODE` to perform a [non-spatial join]

trans-unit.source: In the [processing toolbox](/content/Module_1/en_qgis_start.md#toolbox--toolbars) on the right, search for the tool __"Join attributes by key value"__ and <kbd>double-click</kbd> on it.
trans-unit.target: In the [processing toolbox](/content/Module_1/en_qgis_start.md#toolbox--toolbars) on the right, search for the tool __"Join attributes by key value"__ and <kbd>double-click</kbd> on it.

trans-unit.source: A new window will open. Here we can specify the parameters for the `Join attributes by field value`-tool.
trans-unit.target: A new window will open. Here we can specify the parameters for the `Join attributes by field value`-tool.

trans-unit.source: As "Input layer", select the layer `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB`.
trans-unit.target: As "Input layer", select the layer `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB`.

trans-unit.source: Under "Table field", select `ADM2_PCODE`.
trans-unit.target: Under "Table field", select `ADM2_PCODE`.

trans-unit.source: As "Input layer 2", select `vaccination_coverage_adm2`.
trans-unit.target: As "Input layer 2", select `vaccination_coverage_adm2`.

trans-unit.source: Under "Table field 2", select `adm2_pcode`.
trans-unit.target: Under "Table field 2", select `adm2_pcode`.

trans-unit.source: Under "Layer 2 fields to copy", we can select which columns we want to copy. Click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three dots to the right of the field and select `vaccination_rate_mcv1` and `vaccination_rate_mcv2`. Then, click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Under "Layer 2 fields to copy", we can select which columns we want to copy. Click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three dots to the right of the field and select `vaccination_rate_mcv1` and `vaccination_rate_mcv2`. Then, click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Finally, to execute the algorithm, click on `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Finally, to execute the algorithm, click on `Run`.

trans-unit.source: A new layer called "Joined Layer" will appear in the layers panel. To the right of it, you will see a ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_temp_layer.png) symbol. This symbol indicates that the layer is a temporary scratch layer. This means it will be deleted once you close your QGIS project, even if you save the project.
trans-unit.target: A new layer called "Joined Layer" will appear in the layers panel. To the right of it, you will see a ![](/fig/qgis_3.40_temp_layer.png) symbol. This symbol indicates that the layer is a temporary scratch layer. This means it will be deleted once you close your QGIS project, even if you save the project.

trans-unit.source: __We can save the scratch layer__ by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it and selecting `Make permament...`.
trans-unit.target: __We can save the scratch layer__ by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it and selecting `Make permament...`.

trans-unit.source: A new window will open. Here we need to specify the file location and the layer name.
trans-unit.target: A new window will open. Here we need to specify the file location and the layer name.

trans-unit.source: Leave the `Format` on "GeoPackage".
trans-unit.target: Leave the `Format` on "GeoPackage".

trans-unit.source: Click on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png), navigate to the `data/interim/`-folder and enter a file name such as `tcd_adm2_vacc_coverage`. Click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Click on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png), navigate to the `data/interim/`-folder and enter a file name such as `tcd_adm2_vacc_coverage`. Click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Enter the same name into `Layer name` field (This will be the name of the layer in the layers panel).
trans-unit.target: Enter the same name into `Layer name` field (This will be the name of the layer in the layers panel).

trans-unit.source: Leave the rest as it is and click `Ok`.
trans-unit.target: Leave the rest as it is and click `Ok`.

trans-unit.source: Great! We have added the information on vaccination coverage to our adm2-layer. Now, we can visualise the information by adding a graduated symbology to the layer
trans-unit.target: Great! We have added the information on vaccination coverage to our adm2-layer. Now, we can visualise the information by adding a graduated symbology to the layer

trans-unit.source: Task 6: Visualising the vaccination coverage
trans-unit.target: Task 6: Visualising the vaccination coverage

trans-unit.source: :::{Admonition} Saving your progress :class: tip
trans-unit.target: :::{Admonition} Saving your progress :class: tip

trans-unit.source: Remember to save your project intermittently to keep your progress by clicking ![](/fig/qgis_save_project.png). QGIS is constantly being developed by the open source community and is known to crash from time to time.
trans-unit.target: Remember to save your project intermittently to keep your progress by clicking ![](/fig/qgis_save_project.png). QGIS is constantly being developed by the open source community and is known to crash from time to time.

trans-unit.source: Open the symbology tab via the Properties window for the layer:
trans-unit.target: Open the symbology tab via the Properties window for the layer:

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the `tcd_adm2_vacc_coverage`-layer → `Properties`.
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the `tcd_adm2_vacc_coverage`-layer → `Properties`.

trans-unit.source: Navigate to "Symbology" in the tab section on the left.
trans-unit.target: Navigate to "Symbology" in the tab section on the left.

trans-unit.source: Here we can change the symbology method from `Single Symbol` to `Graduated`.
trans-unit.target: Here we can change the symbology method from `Single Symbol` to `Graduated`.

trans-unit.source: Next, we need to select the value which will be used for the classification. Under `Value`, select the column `vaccination_rate_mcv1` and click on classify. We want to use the Mode `Equal Interval` and use 5 Classes for a first assessment of the vaccination coverage.
trans-unit.target: Next, we need to select the value which will be used for the classification. Under `Value`, select the column `vaccination_rate_mcv1` and click on classify. We want to use the Mode `Equal Interval` and use 5 Classes for a first assessment of the vaccination coverage.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of classified vaccination_rate_mcv1 variable :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of classified vaccination_rate_mcv1 variable :::

trans-unit.source: %% ADD CLASSIFICATION STEPS AND RESULT IMAGE: DONE
trans-unit.target: %% ADD CLASSIFICATION STEPS AND RESULT IMAGE: DONE

trans-unit.source: Task 7: Enriching the Healthsites dataset
trans-unit.target: Task 7: Enriching the Healthsites dataset

trans-unit.source: %% HERE MAYBE HAVE SOME ENTRIES IN THE ADM2 COLUMN USE FRENCH NAMES OR HAVE SOME TYPOS (MAX 2 or 3). BUT SHOW HOW YOU HAVE TO CLEAN DATA TO MAKE THINGS WORK. CHECK THE LOG AND THEN RERUN
trans-unit.target: %% HERE MAYBE HAVE SOME ENTRIES IN THE ADM2 COLUMN USE FRENCH NAMES OR HAVE SOME TYPOS (MAX 2 or 3). BUT SHOW HOW YOU HAVE TO CLEAN DATA TO MAKE THINGS WORK. CHECK THE LOG AND THEN RERUN

trans-unit.source: In this step, we want to enrich the layer containing the healthsites with additional data on the capacity of the healthsites. The layer `tcd_healthsite_capacities.csv` contains information about the bed capacity in the pediatric care unit as well as the cold chain capacity. This information is valuable to identify the capacity of the health sector to treat acute measles cases and coordinate a vaccination campaign.
trans-unit.target: In this step, we want to enrich the layer containing the healthsites with additional data on the capacity of the healthsites. The layer `tcd_healthsite_capacities.csv` contains information about the bed capacity in the pediatric care unit as well as the cold chain capacity. This information is valuable to identify the capacity of the health sector to treat acute measles cases and coordinate a vaccination campaign.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Gathering the information on capacities In a realistic scenario, this data might have been collected during a rapid facility assessment led by the Ministry of Health and Red Cross volunteers. Because data collection was decentralised and partially paper-based, some facility names differ slightly across datasets (e.g., spelling variants, abbreviations). When performing joins, pay attention to such inconsistencies. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Gathering the information on capacities In a realistic scenario, this data might have been collected during a rapid facility assessment led by the Ministry of Health and Red Cross volunteers. Because data collection was decentralised and partially paper-based, some facility names differ slightly across datasets (e.g., spelling variants, abbreviations). When performing joins, pay attention to such inconsistencies. :::

trans-unit.source: Let's import the `tcd_healthsite_capacities.csv` into your QGIS project:
trans-unit.target: Let's import the `tcd_healthsite_capacities.csv` into your QGIS project:

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Delimited Text Layer...`
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Delimited Text Layer...`

trans-unit.source: To the right of the `File name`-field, click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three points and navigate to the `data/input/tcd_healthsite_capacities.csv` file and click `Open`.
trans-unit.target: To the right of the `File name`-field, click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three points and navigate to the `data/input/tcd_healthsite_capacities.csv` file and click `Open`.

trans-unit.source: In the import window, you will see sample data in the sample data field. Take a look at the columns and data available. What kind of data is present in each column?
trans-unit.target: In the import window, you will see sample data in the sample data field. Take a look at the columns and data available. What kind of data is present in each column?

trans-unit.source: Unfortunately, there are no coordinates for the individual healthsites in this datatable. Under `Geometry Definition` select `No geometry (attribute only table)`.
trans-unit.target: Unfortunately, there are no coordinates for the individual healthsites in this datatable. Under `Geometry Definition` select `No geometry (attribute only table)`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Add`. The layer will appear in your layers tab as a data table, but will not be shown in the map canvas.
trans-unit.target: Click `Add`. The layer will appear in your layers tab as a data table, but will not be shown in the map canvas.

trans-unit.source: Let's investigate the capacities table further.
trans-unit.target: Let's investigate the capacities table further.

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the layer and open the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, you can see how many entries the dataset contains (*148 features*)
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, you can see how many entries the dataset contains (*148 features*)

trans-unit.source: The datatable includes a column called `name` which contains the name of the health facilities. These names are the same names that are also stored in the healthsites point layer we imported earlier.
trans-unit.target: The datatable includes a column called `name` which contains the name of the health facilities. These names are the same names that are also stored in the healthsites point layer we imported earlier.

trans-unit.source: This means that we can join both tables using the attribute values of the `name`-column.
trans-unit.target: This means that we can join both tables using the attribute values of the `name`-column.

trans-unit.source: In the [processing toolbox](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_1/en_qgis_start.html#toolbox-toolbars), search for the tool `Join attributes by field value` and open it by <kbd>double-clicking</kbd> on it.
trans-unit.target: In the [processing toolbox](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_1/en_qgis_start.html#toolbox-toolbars), search for the tool `Join attributes by field value` and open it by <kbd>double-clicking</kbd> on it.

trans-unit.source: A new window will open. Here we can specify the parameters for the `Join attributes by field value`-tool.
trans-unit.target: A new window will open. Here we can specify the parameters for the `Join attributes by field value`-tool.

trans-unit.source: As "Input layer", select the layer `hotosm_tcd_health_facilities_points_gpkg`.
trans-unit.target: As "Input layer", select the layer `hotosm_tcd_health_facilities_points_gpkg`.

trans-unit.source: Under "Table field", select `name`.
trans-unit.target: Under "Table field", select `name`.

trans-unit.source: As "Input layer 2", select `tcd_healthsite_capacities`.
trans-unit.target: As "Input layer 2", select `tcd_healthsite_capacities`.

trans-unit.source: Under "Table field 2", select `name`.
trans-unit.target: Under "Table field 2", select `name`.

trans-unit.source: Under "Layer 2 fields to copy", we can select which columns we want to copy. Click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three dots to the right of the field and select `cold_chain`, `measles_vaccination`, `measles_treatment`, `beds_total`, `pediatric_beds`, `staff_total`, and `remarks`. Then, click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Under "Layer 2 fields to copy", we can select which columns we want to copy. Click on the ![](/fig/Three_points.png) three dots to the right of the field and select `cold_chain`, `measles_vaccination`, `measles_treatment`, `beds_total`, `pediatric_beds`, `staff_total`, and `remarks`. Then, click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Finally, to execute the algorithm, click on `Run`. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_8_pub_health_1_join_attr_by_field_value.png
trans-unit.target: Finally, to execute the algorithm, click on `Run`. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_8_pub_health_1_join_attr_by_field_value.png

trans-unit.source: After reviewing the `Log`, we can close the tool-window. A new layer called `Joined layer` should appear in your layers panel. Rename it to `healthsites_points_capacities` and move it to the top.
trans-unit.target: After reviewing the `Log`, we can close the tool-window. A new layer called `Joined layer` should appear in your layers panel. Rename it to `healthsites_points_capacities` and move it to the top.

trans-unit.source: We now have a new point layer with the capacities of relevant healthsites. With this information, we can create a map showing the capacities of the health sector.
trans-unit.target: We now have a new point layer with the capacities of relevant healthsites. With this information, we can create a map showing the capacities of the health sector.

trans-unit.source: Task 8: Cleaning the Healthsite Data
trans-unit.target: Task 8: Cleaning the Healthsite Data

trans-unit.source: %% This step is not necessary
trans-unit.target: %% This step is not necessary

trans-unit.source: Let's take a look at the new layer we just created including the healthsite capacities by opening the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Let's take a look at the new layer we just created including the healthsite capacities by opening the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-Click on the layer and open the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-Click on the layer and open the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: In the attribute table, if you scroll to the right, you will see the new columns with the information we added using the "join attributes by field value"-tool.
trans-unit.target: In the attribute table, if you scroll to the right, you will see the new columns with the information we added using the "join attributes by field value"-tool.

trans-unit.source: Sort the attribute table for the new columns. As you can see, not every feature has information about the capacity.
trans-unit.target: Sort the attribute table for the new columns. As you can see, not every feature has information about the capacity.

trans-unit.source: We can remove the healthsites without additional information, as they are already available in the original dataset.
trans-unit.target: We can remove the healthsites without additional information, as they are already available in the original dataset.

trans-unit.source: Sort the `beds_total` column ascendingly, so the features with "NULL"-values appear at the top.
trans-unit.target: Sort the `beds_total` column ascendingly, so the features with "NULL"-values appear at the top.

trans-unit.source: Click on row column (on the left), and select the first feature. If selected, the feature should appear blue.
trans-unit.target: Click on row column (on the left), and select the first feature. If selected, the feature should appear blue.

trans-unit.source: Now scroll down until you see the first feature with a value different then "NULL" in the `beds_total` column.
trans-unit.target: Now scroll down until you see the first feature with a value different then "NULL" in the `beds_total` column.

trans-unit.source: Hold <kbd>Shift</kbd> and click on the row number of the last feature with the value NULL.
trans-unit.target: Hold <kbd>Shift</kbd> and click on the row number of the last feature with the value NULL.

trans-unit.source: In the toolbar of the attribute table, click on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing Mode`-button to enter the editing mode for the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: In the toolbar of the attribute table, click on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing Mode`-button to enter the editing mode for the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: Next, click on ![](/fig/attribute_table_delete_feature.png) `Delete selected features` to delete the points with no capacity information.
trans-unit.target: Next, click on ![](/fig/attribute_table_delete_feature.png) `Delete selected features` to delete the points with no capacity information.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to save and exit the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to save and exit the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Save the cleaned healthsite capacity layer by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it and selecting `Make permament...`. Select "Geopackage" as the output format and save the layer to the `data/interim/`-folder and enter a file name such as `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities`. Click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Save the cleaned healthsite capacity layer by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it and selecting `Make permament...`. Select "Geopackage" as the output format and save the layer to the `data/interim/`-folder and enter a file name such as `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities`. Click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: %% Adjust the cleaning instructions to decide on cold capacity = NULL: WILL BE DONE IN ONE OF THE FOLLOWING EXERCISES
trans-unit.target: %% Adjust the cleaning instructions to decide on cold capacity = NULL: WILL BE DONE IN ONE OF THE FOLLOWING EXERCISES

trans-unit.source: Our new healthsites point layer now includes only the healthsites for which we received additional data.
trans-unit.target: Our new healthsites point layer now includes only the healthsites for which we received additional data.

trans-unit.source: Task 9: Classifying the Healthsites
trans-unit.target: Task 9: Classifying the Healthsites

trans-unit.source: Now, we can classify the healthsites points to indicate which healthsites have a cold chain in order to store measles vaccines.
trans-unit.target: Now, we can classify the healthsites points to indicate which healthsites have a cold chain in order to store measles vaccines.

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities` and select `Properties`. A new window will open.
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities` and select `Properties`. A new window will open.

trans-unit.source: On the left, navigate to the Symbology-tab. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_8_pub_health_1_classifying_healthpoint_capacity.png
trans-unit.target: On the left, navigate to the Symbology-tab. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_8_pub_health_1_classifying_healthpoint_capacity.png

trans-unit.source: Instead of `Single Symbol`, we will now select `Categorised` as the visualisation method.
trans-unit.target: Instead of `Single Symbol`, we will now select `Categorised` as the visualisation method.

trans-unit.source: As "Value", select `cold_chain`
trans-unit.target: As "Value", select `cold_chain`

trans-unit.source: Next, click on `Classify`.
trans-unit.target: Next, click on `Classify`.

trans-unit.source: If you want, you can adjust the symbology for the classes by double clicking on one.
trans-unit.target: If you want, you can adjust the symbology for the classes by double clicking on one.

trans-unit.source: As we are not interested in `cold_chain` values which equal NULL, we can remove this classification entry by selecting it and clicking on the red minus right next to the `Classify` button.
trans-unit.target: As we are not interested in `cold_chain` values which equal NULL, we can remove this classification entry by selecting it and clicking on the red minus right next to the `Classify` button.

trans-unit.source: Click `Apply`, then close the properties window.
trans-unit.target: Click `Apply`, then close the properties window.

trans-unit.source: Analyze how healthsites with a true `cold_chain` value are distributed.
trans-unit.target: Analyze how healthsites with a true `cold_chain` value are distributed.

trans-unit.source: %% Also remove the NULL values from the categorisation: DONE
trans-unit.target: %% Also remove the NULL values from the categorisation: DONE

trans-unit.source: %% Optionally, add DEM here and style the raster layer. This will be used for a overview map. -> WHAT WAS THE IDEA HERE?
trans-unit.target: %% Optionally, add DEM here and style the raster layer. This will be used for a overview map. -> WHAT WAS THE IDEA HERE?
